channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: New 25k Seater ‘London Colosseum’ Arena Plans To Host Esports Finals
item.content:encoded:
Plans have emerged for a new 25,000-capacity arena, named the ‘London Colosseum,’ which hopes to become the UK’s primary hub for global esports events.

The venue is set to be the home of London’s franchise team in the new NBA Europe League, but there are also plans for it to host world championship boxing and international esports finals.

The planned venue will be located in Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park in Stratford, which also hosts West Ham’s London Stadium and the Copper Box.

The Copper Box has strong ties to esports already, having previously hosted the Rocket League Championship Series (RLCS) Season 5 World Championship and is the venue for next month’s Valorant Masters London.

With a capacity of up to 25k, the London Colosseum would become the UK’s largest indoor arena.

This would see it overtake the 23,500 capacity of the Co-Op Live in Manchester and London’s 20k-seater O2 Arena.

The key to this planned arena is its versatility, according to its architect, Ondrej Chybik:

“By prioritising genuine multifunctionality, the arena and its surrounding public spaces are built to evolve, actively combating the inefficiency of traditional, single-use stadiums.”

There are also plans for the venue to help develop British gaming talent and grassroots esports with the London Colosseum Academy.

This second site will be connected to the arena and feature two 3k-seat venues and an international esports training campus.

The hope is that this will provide pathways for British talents to become elite players while offering educational support.

The Current State of Esports in the UK

The esports scene in the UK is a bit of a mixed bag.

The future of the local League of Legends tournament is up in the air as the NLC is splitting into two separate leagues, one for the UK and Ireland and another for Nordic countries, after the original tournament organiser quit.

Meanwhile, UK games industry trade body Ukie has created a UK Esports Advisory Panel to facilitate discussion with DCMS (the British Government’s Department for Culture, Media and Sport) to strengthen the nation’s esports industry.

And more esports events are coming to the UK as DreamHack Birmingham is confirmed to return in 2027.

What do you think of the esports plans for the London Colosseum? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

item.title: Smolder Nerf Hotfix Added to LoL Patch 26.10
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has quickly released a hotfix to rather heavily nerf Smolder on League of Legends Patch 26.10 just a day into the update.

The ADC had one of the highest win-rates in the game last patch at 54.42%; only Brand had a higher ADC percentage, but that was at a much lower pick rate.

It was also the pick that helped earn Félix ‘xQc’ Lengyel the MVP award in the Jynxzi streamer LoL tournament.

Despite this, Smolder wasn’t initially included in the LoL 26.10 patch notes, while Anivia, Ashe, Naafiri, Shyvana, and Zed all received nerfs.

Now, just a day into the new patch, Riot has announced that the small dragon is receiving nerfs to both his Q and E.

Smolder Nerfs

Riot Games has released the following changes to Smolder in League of Legends:

  • Q – Super Scorcher Breath
    • Damage: 65 / 80 / 95 / 110 / 125 (+130% bonus AD) ⇒ 60 / 75 / 90 / 105 / 120 (+130% bonus AD)
  • W – Achooo!
    • Explosion Physical Damage: 10 – 110 (+65% bonus AD) (+80% AP) ⇒ 10 – 110 (+50% bonus AD) (+80% AP)

LoL lead gameplay designer Matt ‘Phroxzon’ Leung-Harrison took to Twitter to explain these changes, saying:

“We’ve observed his power level continue to grow over the course of the patch. The main factor is his synergy with DFT (Deathfire Touch), and it will continue to be good after the changes.

The Deathfire Touch rune has proven to be too strong, receiving a hotfix nerf last patch and then a nerf to its Damage Per Second in the most recent update.

What champion would you most like to see receive nerfs? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, leakers and dataminers have discovered a possible new feature that will allow LoL players to queue in three roles once the League Next update goes live.

item.title: T1 Peyz Records All-Time LoL Esports Pentakill Record
item.content:encoded:
T1 botlaner Kim ‘Peyz’ Su-hwan is now officially the League of Legends pro with the most pentakills in the esport’s history.

The Korean ADC recorded his 13th career League of Legends pentakill during T1’s LCK victory over Nongshim RedForce.

Peyz has now recorded at least two pentakills for every team he has played for: two for T1, two for JD Gaming, and a staggering nine at Gen.G.

At just 20 years pf age and now playing for the team that has won the last three LoL World Championships, Peyz has a chance to run away with the Pentakills record.

While Peyz is on 13 pentakills, there’s a three-way tie for second with Park ‘Ruler’ Jae-hyuk, Chen ‘GALA’ Wei, and infamous Turkish pro Anıl ‘HolyPhoenix’ Işık all having 12.

They are then closely followed by retired Vietnamese VCS star Nguyễn ‘Celebrity’ Phước Long Hiệp on 11 pentakills, and Yoon ‘Ice’ Sang-hoon as well as Western icons Yiliang ‘Doublelift’ Peng and Martin ‘Rekkles’ Larsson who each have 10.

As for champions, Kai’Sa has almost 100 more pentas than any other champion, with the top five as follows:

  • Kai’Sa (235)
  • Jinx (161)
  • Zeri (137)
  • Aphelios (105)
  • Ezreal (96)

The most from a non-ADC champion is Azir on 50, while all eight support pentakills have come on Pyke.

Thanks to the nine pentas from Peyz, Gen.G is the team with the most recorded pentakills at 18, though they would be far behind the combined 31 if Royal Club and Royal Never Give Up shared stats.

The top five of teams with the most pentakills currently looks like this:

  • Gen.G (18)
  • G2 Esports, Invictus Gaming, and Royal Club (16)
  • eSuba, Royal Never Give Up (15)
  • Team WE (14)
  • Cloud9, Fnatic (13)

What is your favourite pentakill in LoL esports history? Share your memories with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see why content creator Christopher ‘MoistCr1TiKaL’ White Jr. claims the Jynxzi LoL tournament was what the LCS needed.
